signal: Autonomous tender sessions can mark lugs completed without creating implementation files. Sonnet moved 3/5 lugs to completed/ but built zero Python tools. Verification step needed in tender prompt: check target_files exist before marking complete.
rationale: False completions corrupt the lug lifecycle — downstream work assumes dependencies are met. Detection requires manual audit. The tender prompt needs an explicit 'verify target_files exist' gate before status change.

signal: wheel-tender.sh --dry-run still runs Pass 1 (Haiku fleet Claude session, ~2 min, ~$0.50). Dry-run only skips Pass 2 and Pass 3. To check filter result without cost, read the gardener log after a real run or parse filter logic directly. Avoid --dry-run when filter visibility is the goal.
rationale: Wasted $0.52 on a verification pass that gave no new information — the filter result was already visible in the log from the previous run.

signal: False blocker pattern: impl lugs listed impl-lathe-spoke-scoring-v1 as blocked_by, but Lathe was already built (lathe_score.py existed, spoke_lathe.json populated). Blocked_by references are not verified at lug creation. Before treating a blocker as a gate, verify the blocking lug actually exists and is unresolved.
rationale: Would have blocked Phase 2 compass/gardener work indefinitely if not caught during wakeup analysis.

signal: 16/18 spokes had silently broken hooks ($CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR never resolved by CC). Health check passed falsely. Fixed: env-var detection + Tender auto-heal. Fleet remediated in 5 seconds.
rationale: Silent failures across entire fleet. Health check was lying. Assertion-based verification pattern established.

signal: Advisor architecture v2: 9 spoke (Ozi, Marge, Archie, Flo, Will, Jordy, Thad, Mark, Clara) + 5 hub (Octo, Gardener, Spinner, Cartologist, Quartermaster). Hierarchy, lug-message communication, UX four-panel.
rationale: Fundamental architecture for the advisory system.

signal: Wheel Council fully operational: 5 hub advisors built (Gardener/Spinner/Cartologist/Quartermaster/Octo) with nightly post-tend pipeline. Lathe→Spinner and Compass→Cartologist renames complete (zero live refs). Cartologist Flask web interface live on port 8080.
rationale: Hub advisory layer is now a first-class operational system. Closes the longest-running hub evolution epic milestone.

signal: Octo role clarification: Octo is the hub equivalent of Ozi (Chief of Staff), not just a briefing aggregator. Octo v1 (advisory-only) is foundation; Octo v2 must add hub work queue, council dispatch, and escalation routing to make the pipeline operational.
rationale: Fundamental architectural clarification. Octo v1 watches the wheel; Octo v2 steers it. Without v2 the council produces reports but takes no action.

signal: Sub-agent parallelism pattern validated for hub advisor builds: Spinner (sequential, file deps), then Cartologist+Quartermaster+Octo in parallel. Each agent marks its own lug completed and appends to track. 5 agents total consumed ~280k tokens vs estimated 600k+ in single context. Context savings ~55%.
rationale: Establishes the agent dispatch pattern for multi-advisor builds. Reusable for spoke advisor stubs (Archie/Will/Jordy/Mark/Clara) and any future parallel implementation work.

signal: Octo v2 operational: hub work queue (hub_work_queue.json) + council dispatch (council_directives.json) + escalation routing in strategic brief. run_nightly.py now reads Octo's priority order first. Formula: urgency*health_penalty + signal_pressure. Octo = hub Chief of Staff, not just briefing aggregator.
rationale: Completes the hub advisory council operational layer. Gardener now has explicit priority order from Octo instead of Spinner-only. Council members have actionable directive lists each nightly run.

signal: Track integrity check false-positive: closeout-event track entries use event+summary schema (no turn or completed fields). Wakeup check tests d.get(completed) which fails, flagging valid closeout sessions as INTERRUPTED. Fix: check for event==closeout OR completed==true when determining track health.
rationale: False INTERRUPTED detection causes unnecessary recovery prompts at wakeup, erodes trust in the integrity check, and may cause redundant recovery work.

signal: Post-compaction closeout recall hardening pattern: pre-compact.sh writes compacted=true to guard file; user-prompt-submit.sh detects flag and injects one-shot <wai-post-compact> block directing model to read skill file before acting; flag cleared after injection. Prevents silent skill reconstruction from degraded context.
rationale: Context compaction silently strips skill knowledge. Without explicit re-read directive, model reconstructs closeout from memory which degrades. One-shot injection avoids noise on every subsequent turn.
